question_text: A solid metal block is heated. What happens to the movement of particles inside the block?

options.label: B
options.text: The particles vibrate more vigorously around fixed positions

options.label: C
options.text: The particles move to the edges of the block

explanation: When a solid is heated, the thermal energy increases, causing the particles to vibrate more rapidly around their fixed positions. This increased vibration is what we observe as an increase in temperature.
